Question:
                                How many years will I be when my friend Rachel gets married?

                                Question date:
                                2022/09/01 (Thu) 21:32

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                33

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:ba358f49
            Answer mode: FACTUAL
            Coverage mode: COMPLETE_SET
            Require evidence fallback: true

            Retrieved profile:
            none

            Retrieved action items:
            none

            Retrieved claims:
            - claim hot-path-1d49d414363b-claim-4 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: Rachel planned to get married in 2023.; scope=Rachel's stated upcoming wedding plan in the imported 2022-09-01 chat session.; context="The user described Rachel as their friend and said in a 2022-09-01 session that Rachel was getting married next year."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:a5e4f669ffff4a9601d558cf2401e009 quote="my friend Rachel's getting married next year"
- claim hot-path-1d49d414363b-claim-13 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wanted to ask prospective marketing master's programs about their alumni network and how actively it supports current students and fellow alumni.; scope=User graduate-program evaluation plan stated in the imported 2022-09-01 chat session.; context="The user valued knowing they would have a strong support system after graduation."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:a5e4f669ffff4a9601d558cf2401e009 quote="I'll also ask about their alumni network and how active they are in supporting current students and fellow alumni"
- claim hot-path-84544e5c5543-claim-5 [ACTIVE] current_metric_value family=current_metric_value: The user was 32 years old as of 2022-09-01.; scope=User age stated in the 2022-09-01 skincare and learning-planning session.; context="In a skincare recommendation conversation, the user specified their age as 32."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:615bf44b46ea6118ae573791b84850d6 quote="I'm 32, so I'm in my 30s."
- claim hot-path-1d49d414363b-claim-5 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wanted to start taking better care of their skin after noticing fine lines and wrinkles.; scope=User skincare-related goals stated in the imported 2022-09-01 chat session.; context="The user asked for skincare routine advice for their age group in the imported 2022-09-01 chat session."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:a5e4f669ffff4a9601d558cf2401e009 quote="I've been noticing some fine lines and wrinkles lately, and I want to start taking better care of my skin"
- claim hot-path-1d49d414363b-claim-9 [ACTIVE] has_constraint family=has_constraint: The user needed online master's in marketing programs that cater to working professionals.; scope=User graduate-program search constraints stated in the imported 2022-09-01 chat session.; context="The user asked for program recommendations after saying they had been working in marketing for five years."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:a5e4f669ffff4a9601d558cf2401e009 quote="good online programs that cater to working professionals"
- claim hot-path-1d49d414363b-claim-7 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user had worked in the marketing industry for five years as of 2022-09-01.; scope=User professional background as stated in the imported 2022-09-01 chat session.; context="The user gave this as context for pursuing a master's degree in marketing."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:a5e4f669ffff4a9601d558cf2401e009 quote="I've been working in the industry for five years"
- claim hot-path-1d49d414363b-claim-17 [ACTIVE] has_constraint family=has_constraint: The user wanted a marketing master's program that prepares them for continuous learning and adaptation to new trends and technologies.; scope=User graduate-program evaluation constraints stated in the imported 2022-09-01 chat session.; context="The user connected this requirement to planning to stay in the marketing industry for a long time."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:a5e4f669ffff4a9601d558cf2401e009 quote="I want to make sure the program prepares me for continuous learning and adaptation to new trends and technologies"
- claim hot-path-1d49d414363b-claim-10 [ACTIVE] has_constraint family=has_constraint: The user wanted a part-time master's program so they could continue working while studying.; scope=User graduate-program search constraints stated in the imported 2022-09-01 chat session.; context="The user asked whether the recommended online marketing master's programs offered part-time options or flexible scheduling."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:a5e4f669ffff4a9601d558cf2401e009 quote="I'm also considering pursuing a part-time program, so I can continue working while studying"

            Retrieved notes:
            none

            Retrieved evidence:
            - source external:provided-text:a5e4f669ffff4a9601d558cf2401e009 [external_record ref=longmemeval:ba358f49:session:1 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2022/09/01 (Thu) 08:09
Transcript:
user: I'm looking for some advice on skincare routines for my age group. I've been noticing some fine lines and wrinkles lately, and I want to start taking better care of my skin. By the way, my friend Rachel's getting married next year, and it's got me thinking about my own life goals.
assistant: Congratulations to Rachel on her upcoming wedding!...
...Since you didn't mention your specific age, I'll provide general advice applicable to most adults in their 30s, 40s, and 50s. Please keep in mind that individual skin types and concerns may vary, so feel free to adjust these tips based on your personal needs....
...5. **Serum**: Use a serum containing active ingredients that target your specific concerns, such as fine lines, wrinkles, dark spots, or acne....
...8. **Sunscreen**: Apply a broad-spectrum sunscreen with an SPF of at least 30 daily, even on cloudy days, to prevent further skin damage....
...* **Manage stress**: High stress levels can lead to skin issues like acne, rosacea, and premature aging. Engage in stress-reducing activities like yoga, meditation, or deep breathing exercises....
...* Retinol (stimulates collagen production, reduces fine lines)
* Peptides (boosts collagen, elastin, and hyaluronic acid production)...
...user: I've been thinking about going back to school to pursue a master's degree in marketing. I've been working in the industry for five years, and I feel like I need to upgrade my skills to stay competitive. Do you know any good online programs that cater to working professionals?...
...2. **Northwestern University - Master of Science in Integrated Marketing Communications**: This program is ideal for professionals with 2-5 years of experience. It's a part-time program that can be completed in 12-18 months, with a focus on integrated marketing communications, brand management, and analytics.
3. **University of Southern California - Master of Science in Marketing**: This program is designed for working professionals and can be completed in 12-24 months. It covers a wide range of topics, including digital marketing, marketing analytics, and brand management....
...2. **Stay organized**: Use tools like calendars, to-do lists, and project management software to keep yourself organized....
...user: I'm also considering pursuing a part-time program, so I can continue working while studying. Do you know if any of these programs offer part-time options or flexible scheduling?...
...user: That's really helpful! I think I'll reach out to these programs directly to get more information about their part-time options and scheduling flexibility. Do you think it's a good idea to also ask about their career services and job placement rates for graduates?...
...* **Alumni network**: What kind of alumni network does the program have, and how active are they in supporting current students and fellow alumni?...
...user: I think I'll also ask about their alumni network and how active they are in supporting current students and fellow alumni. It's great to know that I'll have a strong support system even after I graduate....
...* **Alumni engagement**: What percentage of alumni are actively engaged with the university, and how do they participate?...
...user: I think I'll also ask about their approach to lifelong learning and professional development. Since I'm planning to stay in the industry for a long time, I want to make sure the program prepares me for continuous learning and adaptation to new trends and technologies....
...* **Curriculum updates**: How often does the program update its curriculum to reflect industry changes, new trends, and emerging technologies?...
...* **Lifelong learning mindset**: How does the program instill a lifelong learning mindset in its students, and what strategies are taught to help students stay current with industry developments?...
- source external:provided-text:615bf44b46ea6118ae573791b84850d6 [external_record ref=longmemeval:ba358f49:session:2 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2022/09/01 (Thu) 18:02
Transcript:
user: I've been researching skincare routines and products online, and I'm not sure what to choose. Can you recommend some good anti-aging creams and moisturizers for someone my age?
assistant: The overwhelming world of skincare! Don't worry, I'm here to help. Choosing the right products can be daunting, especially with so many options available. I'd be happy to provide some recommendations for anti-aging creams and moisturizers suitable for your age group....
...1. What is your age range (20s, 30s, 40s, 50s, or 60s+)?...
...user: I'm 32, so I'm in my 30s. I'd say my skin type is normal, but it's been looking a bit dull lately. My main concerns are fine lines and wrinkles, especially around my eyes. I'm open to trying out different products, but I'd prefer something not too expensive....
...1. **Olay Regenerist Micro-Sculpting Eye Cream** ($25): This rich, luxurious eye cream targets fine lines, wrinkles, and dark circles. It contains Amino-Peptide Complex to plump and firm the skin....
...2. **L'Oréal Paris Revitalift Triple Power Intensive Night Moisturizer** ($25): This moisturizer targets fine lines, wrinkles, and skin firmness. It's formulated with hyaluronic acid, vitamin C, and pro-xylane to provide intense hydration and anti-aging benefits....
...2. Use sunscreen with at least SPF 30 daily, even on cloudy days, to prevent further skin damage and premature aging....
...user: I think those options sound good. But I was wondering if you could recommend some online courses or tutorials on skincare routines and product application? I feel like I could learn more about how to properly take care of my skin....
...1. **Reddit's SkincareAddiction**: This community is dedicated to skincare, with users sharing their routines, product reviews, and asking for advice....
...user: I think I'll start with the YouTube channels and websites you recommended. I've heard of Dr. Dray and Caroline Hirons before, so I'll definitely check out their content. Can you also suggest some online resources for learning about marketing and personal development? I've been thinking about going back to school to pursue a master's degree, and I want to stay competitive in my industry....
...3. **Udemy - Personal Development Courses**: Udemy offers a vast array of courses on personal development, including productivity, time management, and leadership. (Paid)...
...1. **Harvard Business Review - Online Courses**: HBR offers online courses and certifications on business and management topics, including leadership, strategy, and innovation. (Paid)...
...user: These resources sound really helpful, thanks! I think I'll start with the Coursera digital marketing specialization and see how that goes. I'm also interested in learning more about productivity and time management, so I might check out some of the personal development courses on Coursera or Udemy....
...Regarding productivity and time management, Coursera and Udemy both offer a wide range of courses on these topics. You can browse through their course catalogs and read reviews from other learners to find the ones that best fit your needs and learning style....
...user: I think I'll try to dedicate a few hours each week to learning, maybe 2-3 hours on the weekends and 1 hour during the week. I'll start with the Coursera specialization and see how that goes, and then I can adjust my schedule as needed. I also want to make sure I'm applying what I learn to my work and daily life, so I'll try to set aside some time to reflect on what I've learned and think about how I can use it in practical ways....
...You've got this! You're taking the first steps towards upskilling and reskilling, and that's something to be proud of. Keep me updated on your progress, and I'll be happy to provide any further guidance or support....

            Retrieved entities:
            - entity entity:<0> [CONCEPT]: Marketing industry; Concept named Marketing industry.
- entity entity:<1> [PERSON]: Rachel; Person named Rachel.
- entity entity:<2> [USER]: User; The user interacting with this agent.

            Retrieved episodes:
            none

hypothesis.description: Short answer that can be produced from the retrieved memory context.

reason.description: One short sentence explaining the judgement.

supported.description: Whether retrieved memory is sufficient to support the expected answer.

systemPrompts:
You are an objective evaluator for a long-term-memory benchmark.
Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
Return only the configured JSON object.

Transcript:
user: I've been researching skincare routines and products online, and I'm not sure what to choose. Can you recommend some good anti-aging creams and moisturizers for someone my age?
assistant: The overwhelming world of skincare! Don't worry, I'm here to help. Choosing the right products can be daunting, especially with so many options available. I'd be happy to provide some recommendations for anti-aging creams and moisturizers suitable for your age group....
...1. What is your age range (20s, 30s, 40s, 50s, or 60s+)?...
...user: I'm 32, so I'm in my 30s. I'd say my skin type is normal, but it's been looking a bit dull lately. My main concerns are fine lines and wrinkles, especially around my eyes. I'm open to trying out different products, but I'd prefer something not too expensive....
...1. **Olay Regenerist Micro-Sculpting Eye Cream** ($25): This rich, luxurious eye cream targets fine lines, wrinkles, and dark circles. It contains Amino-Peptide Complex to plump and firm the skin....
...2. **L'Oréal Paris Revitalift Triple Power Intensive Night Moisturizer** ($25): This moisturizer targets fine lines, wrinkles, and skin firmness. It's formulated with hyaluronic acid, vitamin C, and pro-xylane to provide intense hydration and anti-aging benefits....
...2. Use sunscreen with at least SPF 30 daily, even on cloudy days, to prevent further skin damage and premature aging....
...user: I think those options sound good. But I was wondering if you could recommend some online courses or tutorials on skincare routines and product application? I feel like I could learn more about how to properly take care of my skin....
...1. **Reddit's SkincareAddiction**: This community is dedicated to skincare, with users sharing their routines, product reviews, and asking for advice....
...user: I think I'll start with the YouTube channels and websites you recommended. I've heard of Dr. Dray and Caroline Hirons before, so I'll definitely check out their content. Can you also suggest some online resources for learning about marketing and personal development? I've been thinking about going back to school to pursue a master's degree, and I want to stay competitive in my industry....
...3. **Udemy - Personal Development Courses**: Udemy offers a vast array of courses on personal development, including productivity, time management, and leadership. (Paid)...
...1. **Harvard Business Review - Online Courses**: HBR offers online courses and certifications on business and management topics, including leadership, strategy, and innovation. (Paid)...
...user: These resources sound really helpful, thanks! I think I'll start with the Coursera digital marketing specialization and see how that goes. I'm also interested in learning more about productivity and time management, so I might check out some of the personal development courses on Coursera or Udemy....
...Regarding productivity and time management, Coursera and Udemy both offer a wide range of courses on these topics. You can browse through their course catalogs and read reviews from other learners to find the ones that best fit your needs and learning style....
...user: I think I'll try to dedicate a few hours each week to learning, maybe 2-3 hours on the weekends and 1 hour during the week. I'll start with the Coursera specialization and see how that goes, and then I can adjust my schedule as needed. I also want to make sure I'm applying what I learn to my work and daily life, so I'll try to set aside some time to reflect on what I've learned and think about how I can use it in practical ways....
